Report of Board Counsel - Education and Volunteer
16 Services (16A-5337)
17 [Jacqueline Wolfgang, Esquire, Regulatory Counsel,
18 stated 16A-5337, regarding opioid education,
19 volunteer services, and organ donation continuing
20 education, was published as proposed in November
21 2025. The Board did not receive any public comments
22 or any comments from the Independent Regulatory
23 Review Commission (IRRC). IRRC indicated the
24 regulation would be deemed approved if the Board did
25 not make any changes to the final rulemaking. Ms.
Sargent's Court Reporting Service, Inc.
(814) 536-8908

1 2
1 Wolfgang prepared the final rulemaking for the
2 Board's review and recommended it move forward
3 without any changes.

5 requirements going into effect on May 1, 2026, she
6 was working with Board Counsel to put a notice on the
7 Board's website. Licensees will have five years to

correct_facts.title: Final rulemaking 16A-5337 on continuing education requirements
correct_facts.description: Regulation 16A-5337 covering opioid education, volunteer services, and organ donation continuing education drew no public or IRRC comments; Regulatory Counsel prepared the final rulemaking and recommended it move forward without changes, with organ donation CE effective May 1, 2026.
correct_facts.quote:
Wolfgang prepared the final rulemaking for the
2 Board's review and recommended it move forward
3 without any changes.

commentary: instrument='rule' (a numbered regulation, 16A-5337); stage='adopted' because it is the FINAL rulemaking moving forward unchanged after the comment period closed, not a fresh proposal; topic='CME' since the regulation sets continuing-education requirements.
